executive_summary: Total store sales for the week ending 23 May 2026 came in at 369,339.93 inc VAT (340,453.64 ex VAT), with an overall gross margin of 17.71%.
Total waste for the week was 6,831.41, representing 2.01% of sales ex VAT, which is a material drag on profitability and requires immediate departmental focus.

The fresh departments (Beef, Lamb, Poultry, Fish) are all reporting negative or negligible cost of sales figures, suggesting stock movement anomalies or incomplete purchase data that must be reconciled before true margin can be assessed.
Deli waste at 1,250.61 is the single largest waste contributor, followed closely by Provisions at 1,223.48 and Produce at 1,172.33, collectively accounting for over 50% of total store waste.

Provisions is reporting a cost of sales of 10,621.95 against sales of 25,994.34, yielding a scan margin of 19.96% versus a reported P&L margin of 59.14%, indicating a significant and unexplained variance that requires urgent investigation.

Cigarettes is reporting a margin of 100% in the P&L with no purchases or stock movement recorded, which is clearly a data integrity issue; the scan margin of 12.78% is the only reliable reference point.

The Produce department delivered strong sales of 41,226.68, the highest of all departments with available sales data, though waste of 1,172.33 (approximately 2.84% of Produce sales) needs to be actively managed.

Lamb is showing a negative cost of sales of -96.26, and Poultry a negative cost of sales of -133.59, both indicating closing stock values exceeded opening stock plus any recorded purchases, which points to either over-receiving records or missing purchase entries.
biggest_wins: Produce delivered the highest sales volume of any department at 41,226.68 and maintained a reported P&L margin close to scan margin of 19.27%, suggesting disciplined stock management outside of waste.
Deli achieved sales of 8,786.34 with a scan margin of 42.98%, indicating strong theoretical profitability in this department when waste is brought under control.
Provisions generated the second-highest sales figure at 25,994.34 with a scan margin of 19.96%, demonstrating healthy volume throughput in ambient grocery.
Fish maintained a positive cost of sales of 167.39 with a scan margin of 15.80%, indicating that stock levels are being actively managed relative to prior weeks.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes P&L margin is recorded at 100% with zero purchases and no stock figures, representing a clear data capture failure; this department had sales of 7,821.26 and must be reconciled immediately as it is distorting the overall margin picture.

Provisions shows a stark divergence between its P&L margin of 59.14% and its scan margin of 19.96%, suggesting either a stock write-down, inflated opening stock, or significant missing purchase data; the 10,621.95 cost of sales on 25,994.34 sales requires line-by-line review.

Deli waste of 1,250.61 is the highest of any single department and, combined with a gap between a 98.75% P&L margin and a 42.98% scan margin, strongly indicates missing purchase data alongside a real waste problem.
Produce waste of 1,172.33 represents approximately 2.84% of Produce sales and is a significant margin risk given the high volume throughput of this department.

Lamb and Poultry both have negative cost of sales (-96.26 and -133.59 respectively), which cannot reflect genuine trading activity; purchase data is either absent or incorrectly posted and must be corrected before week-end accounts are finalised.

Fish waste of 874.05 is disproportionately high relative to the department's cost of sales of 167.39, suggesting waste is not being fully captured or that the cost of sales figure is understated due to missing purchases.
Bread and Cakes waste of 916.99 is a significant standalone figure and, while this department does not appear separately in the P&L, this level of waste requires an ordering and markdown review.

Total waste of 6,831.41 at 2.01% of sales represents a meaningful profit leakage across the store; if brought to a target of 1.00%, approximately 3,400 in additional gross profit would be recovered weekly.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Sales of 7,821.26 recorded but no purchases, opening stock, or closing stock data present; P&L margin of 100% is not valid. Scan margin of 12.78% is the only usable reference. Data must be corrected.

Beef: Negative or absent sales data in P&L; cost of sales of 1,601.54 driven by opening stock of 11,543.00 closing at 9,941.46. Scan margin of 19.85% provides the margin benchmark. Waste of 282.12 to be monitored.

Lamb: Negative cost of sales of -96.26 recorded, indicating closing stock of 1,402.26 exceeds opening stock of 1,306.00 with no purchases recorded. Scan margin of 23.67% is healthy if data is corrected. Waste of 108.81 is manageable.
Pork: Cost of sales of 589.51 with opening stock of 1,478.00 and closing stock of 888.49 and no purchases recorded. Scan margin of 22.20% is solid. Waste of 166.33 should be monitored against volume.
Bacon and Loose SRP: Low cost of sales of 99.65 with a scan margin of 10.38%, which is the weakest scan margin across all fresh departments. Waste of 127.66 should be reviewed against sales volume.

Poultry: Negative cost of sales of -133.59 with closing stock of 3,094.59 exceeding opening stock of 2,961.00 and no purchases recorded. Scan margin of 16.58% is below the fresh department average. Waste of 190.20 noted.

Fish: Cost of sales of 167.39 with scan margin of 15.80%, the lowest among the fresh proteins. Waste of 874.05 is disproportionately high and is the primary risk in this department; ordering and date rotation must be reviewed urgently.

Produce: Strongest sales performance at 41,226.68 with a scan margin of 19.27%. Waste of 1,172.33 is the third-highest in the store and equates to approximately 2.84% of department sales; markdown strategy and ordering discipline need review.

Deli: Sales of 8,786.34 with a high scan margin of 42.98% indicating strong theoretical profitability. However, waste of 1,250.61 is the single largest waste figure in the store and is significantly eroding actual returns. Production planning must be addressed.

Provisions: Sales of 25,994.34 with a scan margin of 19.96%. The P&L margin of 59.14% versus scan of 19.96% is a major variance that cannot be explained by normal trading; opening stock of 10,872.00 closing at 250.05 suggests a near-total stock clearance or a data posting error. Waste of 1,223.48 also requires explanation.
Dairy (waste only): Waste of 372.62 recorded with no separate P&L entry visible. Date management and ordering should be reviewed to reduce this figure.
Bread and Cakes (waste only): Waste of 916.99 is the second-highest non-fresh waste figure after Deli and Provisions. End-of-day markdown procedures and ordering levels should be audited.
Frozen (waste only): Waste of 146.22 recorded. While relatively low, the root cause should be confirmed, particularly around freezer integrity and stock rotation.
action_plan: Immediately reconcile Cigarettes department data: locate and post all missing purchase and stock figures so that a valid P&L margin can be calculated for week-end reporting.

Investigate and correct the Provisions P&L anomaly as a priority: review the closing stock figure of 250.05 against an opening of 10,872.00 to determine whether this reflects a genuine stock count, a write-down, or a posting error, and reconcile against the scan margin of 19.96%.

Post missing purchase data for Lamb, Poultry, and all departments showing negative or zero cost of sales; engage the buying or accounts team to confirm all supplier invoices have been received and posted for the week.

Conduct a Deli production review: waste of 1,250.61 against sales of 8,786.34 is unsustainable. Reduce production volumes on slow-moving lines, implement a structured end-of-day markdown programme, and review preparation timing to align with peak footfall windows.

Implement a Fish ordering and date rotation review immediately: waste of 874.05 with a cost of sales of only 167.39 is inconsistent and suggests either under-recording of purchases or significant over-ordering. A full stock and date check should be conducted at start of next trading week.

Review Produce ordering levels and markdown procedures to address waste of 1,172.33; introduce daily waste tracking at department level and set a target of reducing Produce waste to below 2.00% of Produce sales.

Address Bread and Cakes waste of 916.99 by reviewing daily order quantities, introducing a structured end-of-day clearance markdown at a minimum of two hours before close, and confirming the bakery order schedule reflects current sales patterns.

Review Bacon and Loose SRP scan margin of 10.38%, the weakest fresh scan margin in the store; confirm product mix, pricing accuracy, and whether any promotional activity is compressing margin beyond the agreed promotional plan.

Set a store waste target of 1.50% of sales for the coming week, representing a reduction from 2.01%, and hold a brief daily waste review with department managers covering the top five waste contributors.

Ensure all department stock counts for the following week are completed accurately and on time to prevent a repeat of the data integrity issues seen this week, particularly in departments where negative cost of sales figures have been generated.

Review Poultry scan margin of 16.58% and confirm whether promotional pricing, shrink, or supplier cost changes are responsible for the below-average margin, and take corrective action on ordering or pricing as appropriate.

Prepare a full data quality checklist for the next weekly P&L review, flagging any department where P&L margin deviates from scan margin by more than 10 percentage points, to ensure early identification of posting errors before the report is finalised.
